meta.title: Te Pā Governance Comparison
meta.subtitle: Current governance state vs rhizome-integrated target across six systems axes
meta.kaupapa: Each axis cites a public reference. Scores are qualitative readings against public evidence, not synthetic metrics.
meta.scale_note: 0 = absent, 100 = fully realised. Values are working readings that community annotation can revise.

comparison.interpretation: Current authority sits mainly with formal governance nodes; the target model distributes bounded response capacity into pathways.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

comparison.interpretation: Feedback exists but does not consistently reach decision nodes in a visible form.
Slowdowns are hidden inside informal process; the target model makes delays legible and therefore governable.
The biggest gap is not input but closure — whether people can see what changed after they spoke.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

comparison.interpretation: Rule foundations are already comparatively strong because Māori control is explicit; the task is to preserve this while scaling pathway autonomy.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

comparison.interpretation: The rhizome target is much stronger on emergent pathway growth and distributed initiative.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)
